title: Catapodium loliaceum
intro: Catapodium loliaceum, also known as blue grass or small cat's tail, is a perennial grass-like plant belonging to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Catapodium loliaceum include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Catapodium loliaceum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural expectorant to help relieve coughs and congestion.
taxonomy: Catapodium loliaceum is classified as a species within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Catapodium, which is a group of plants commonly found in the Mediterranean region. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of grasses, including many species of grasses and wildflowers. Catapodium loliaceum is a perennial plant, meaning it grows year after year and can form dense stands. The classification of Catapodium loliaceum is also reflected in its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system developed by Carl Linnaeus.

Catapodium loliaceum's traditional use in folk medicine highlights its potential as a natural remedy for various ailments.
properties: Catapodium loliaceum, a Mediterranean plant species, has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. Research suggests that extracts from the plant may have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects.
